ucs.title: [LLM] PROMETHIUM/StrongPity trojanized-installer → masqueraded service drop chain
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical StrongPity intrusion: a victim runs what looks like a legitimate installer (Notepad++, WinRAR, 7-Zip, CCleaner, FireFox, VPN client, IDE tooling) downloaded from an attacker-controlled watering hole, the installer side-loads a secondary PE into a user-writable directory (%AppData%, %ProgramData%, %Public%) and registers it as a Windows service for persistence. PROMETHIUM has used this exact T1189 → T1204.002 → T1543.003 chain for a decade against Turkish/Kurdish targets, with code-signed droppers (T1587.002/T1553.002) carrying real software bundled with the implant.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as install_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("npp.*Installer.exe","notepad++*.exe","winrar*.exe","7z*.exe","CCleaner*.exe","Firefox*Setup*.exe","VPNSetup*.exe","setup.exe","install.exe") OR Processes.parent_process IN ("*\\Downloads\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_id _time
| `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")`
| rename parent_process_id as installer_pid
| join type=inner dest installer_pid [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Services where Services.action=created AND (Services.service_path_name IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Temp\\*") OR NOT Services.service_path_name IN ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\*","C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*","C:\\Program Files\\*","C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*")) by Services.dest Services.service_name Services.service_path_name Services.process_id _time
    | `drop_dm_object_name("Services")`
    | rename process_id as installer_pid _time as svc_install_time
]
| eval delay_sec=svc_install_time-install_time
| where delay_sec >= 0 AND delay_sec <= 600
| table install_time svc_install_time delay_sec dest user parent_process_name service_name service_path_name
ucs.kql:
// PROMETHIUM / StrongPity — trojanized installer drops masqueraded service binary
let Lookback = 7d;
let WindowSec = 600;            // 10-min correlation: installer → service install
let InstallerNamePatterns = dynamic(["npp","notepad++","winrar","7z","7-zip","ccleaner","firefox","setup","install","vpn","truecrypt","veracrypt","putty","wireshark","teamviewer","anydesk"]);
let UserWritablePaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\"]);
let Installers =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
         or FolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
    | where FileName has_any (InstallerNamePatterns)
    | project InstallerTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              InstallerName = FileName, InstallerPath = FolderPath,
              InstallerCmd = ProcessCommandLine, InstallerSHA256 = SHA256;
// Service installation event (ETW: services.exe ServiceInstalled / 7045-equivalent)
let ServiceInstalls =
    DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("ServiceInstalled","PowerShellCommand")
    | where ActionType == "ServiceInstalled"
         or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "sc.exe" and InitiatingProcessCommandLine has "create")
    | extend ImagePath = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).ServiceImagePath),
             SvcName   = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).ServiceName)
    | where isnotempty(ImagePath)
    | where ImagePath has_any (UserWritablePaths)
         or not(ImagePath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\")
            and not(ImagePath startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64\")
            and not(ImagePath startswith @"C:\Program Files\")
            and not(ImagePath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\")
    | project SvcInstallTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, SvcName, ImagePath,
              SvcInstaller = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              SvcInstallerPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              SvcInstallerCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
Installers
| join kind=inner ServiceInstalls on DeviceId
| where SvcInstallTime between (InstallerTime .. InstallerTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SvcInstallTime, InstallerTime)
| project InstallerTime, SvcInstallTime, DelaySec, DeviceName, AccountName,
          InstallerName, InstallerPath, InstallerSHA256,
          SvcName, ImagePath, SvcInstaller
| order by InstallerTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// PROMETHIUM / StrongPity — installer-spawned service install (Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688 + 7045)
let Lookback = 7d;
let WindowSec = 600;
let InstallerNamePatterns = dynamic(["npp","notepad++","winrar","7z","7-zip","ccleaner","firefox","setup.exe","install.exe","vpn","truecrypt","veracrypt","putty","wireshark","teamviewer","anydesk"]);
let UserWritablePaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\"]);
let Installers =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
         or NewProcessName has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
    | where NewProcessName has_any (InstallerNamePatterns)
    | project InstallerTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              InstallerImage = NewProcessName, InstallerCmd = CommandLine,
              InstallerPid = NewProcessId;
// 7045 = A service was installed in the system
let ServiceInstalls =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 7045
    | extend SvcName  = tostring(extract(@"Service Name:\s*(\S.*)", 1, EventData)),
             ImagePath = tostring(extract(@"Service File Name:\s*(\S.*)", 1, EventData))
    | where isnotempty(ImagePath)
    | where ImagePath has_any (UserWritablePaths)
         or (not(ImagePath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\")
             and not(ImagePath startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64\")
             and not(ImagePath startswith @"C:\Program Files\")
             and not(ImagePath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\"))
    | project SvcInstallTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, SvcName, ImagePath;
Installers
| join kind=inner ServiceInstalls on Computer
| where SvcInstallTime between (InstallerTime .. InstallerTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SvcInstallTime, InstallerTime)
| project InstallerTime, SvcInstallTime, DelaySec, Computer, Account,
          InstallerImage, InstallerCmd, SvcName, ImagePath
| order by InstallerTime desc
ucs.rationale: Generic 'installer in Downloads' alerts and generic 'service installed' alerts both produce noise. The StrongPity tradecraft pivot is the same user-mode installer process that created the side-dropped PE then immediately registers it as a service in a non-System32 path within seconds. Joining the installer execution to the 7045/ServiceInstalled event on the same host within a 10-minute window repr

ucs.title: [LLM] PROMETHIUM service masquerading legitimate Windows binary names from non-System32 path
ucs.description: Catches StrongPity's persistence step in isolation when the installer chain UC misses the front half: a Windows service is installed whose ServiceName or ImagePath filename collides with a known Windows-resident binary (spoolsv, prntwsv, wndprbe, audiodg, lsass, svchost) but whose ImagePath sits in %AppData%, %ProgramData% or %Public%. PROMETHIUM specifically reuses near-namesake service binaries (T1036.005) to blend with legitimate svchost/print-spooler entries on Turkish-language Windows builds.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Services.service_path_name) as image_path values(Services.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Services where Services.action=created by Services.dest Services.service_name Services.service_dll
| `drop_dm_object_name("Services")`
| eval svc_basename=lower(replace(service_path_name,".*\\\\",""))
| eval svc_basename=replace(svc_basename,"\".*","")
| eval masq_match=case(
    match(svc_basename,"^(spoolsv|prntwsv|wndprbe|svchost|services|lsass|winlogon|csrss|audiodg|sihost|taskhostw|wuauclt|smss)\.exe$"),"true",
    match(service_name,"(?i)(spool|print|wndprbe|defender|update|audio|task|win)"),"name_collision",
    1=1,"false")
| eval path_lower=lower(image_path)
| eval suspicious_path=if(match(path_lower,"\\\\(appdata|programdata|users\\\\public|temp)\\\\") OR NOT match(path_lower,"^(c:\\\\windows\\\\system32|c:\\\\windows\\\\syswow64|c:\\\\program files)"),"true","false")
| where (masq_match!="false") AND suspicious_path="true"
| table first_seen dest service_name svc_basename image_path user masq_match
ucs.kql:
// PROMETHIUM / StrongPity — service masquerading common Windows binary, ImagePath outside System32
let Lookback = 14d;
let KnownWindowsBinaries = dynamic(["spoolsv.exe","prntwsv.exe","wndprbe.exe","svchost.exe","services.exe","lsass.exe","winlogon.exe","csrss.exe","audiodg.exe","sihost.exe","taskhostw.exe","wuauclt.exe","smss.exe","dllhost.exe","conhost.exe"]);
let TrustedPaths = dynamic([@"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\", @"c:\program files\", @"c:\program files (x86)\"]);
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where ActionType == "ServiceInstalled"
| extend ImagePath = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).ServiceImagePath)),
         SvcName   = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).ServiceName),
         SvcDisplay = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).ServiceDisplayName)
| extend SvcBase = tostring(extract(@"([^\\\"]+\.exe)", 1, ImagePath))
| where isnotempty(ImagePath)
// Masquerade: filename collides with a known Windows binary
| where SvcBase in~ (KnownWindowsBinaries)
     or SvcDisplay has_any ("Print Spooler","Windows Defender","Windows Update","Audio","Background","Print Service","Spooler")
// ImagePath sits outside trusted directories
| where not(ImagePath has_any (TrustedPaths))
| where ImagePath has_any (@"\appdata\", @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\temp\", @"\roaming\")
     or not(ImagePath startswith @"c:\windows\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, SvcName, SvcDisplay, ImagePath, SvcBase,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// PROMETHIUM / StrongPity — masqueraded service install via 7045
let Lookback = 14d;
let KnownWindowsBinaries = dynamic(["spoolsv.exe","prntwsv.exe","wndprbe.exe","svchost.exe","services.exe","lsass.exe","winlogon.exe","csrss.exe","audiodg.exe","sihost.exe","taskhostw.exe","wuauclt.exe","smss.exe","dllhost.exe","conhost.exe"]);
let TrustedPaths = dynamic([@"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\", @"c:\program files\", @"c:\program files (x86)\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 7045
| extend SvcName    = tostring(extract(@"(?ms)Service Name:\s*(\S.*?)\r?\n", 1, EventData)),
         SvcDisplay = tostring(extract(@"(?ms)Display Name:\s*(\S.*?)\r?\n", 1, EventData)),
         ImagePath  = tolower(tostring(extract(@"(?ms)Service File Name:\s*(\S.*?)\r?\n", 1, EventData)))
| extend SvcBase = tostring(extract(@"([^\\\"]+\.exe)", 1, ImagePath))
| where isnotempty(ImagePath)
| where SvcBase in~ (KnownWindowsBinaries)
     or SvcDisplay has_any ("Print Spooler","Windows Defender","Windows Update","Audio","Background","Print ServiceSid","Spooler")
| where not(ImagePath has_any (TrustedPaths))
| where ImagePath has_any (@"\appdata\", @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\temp\", @"\roaming\")
     or not(ImagePath startswith @"c:\windows\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, SubjectUserName, SvcName, SvcDisplay, ImagePath, SvcBase
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: PROMETHIUM / StrongPity Masqueraded Service Install Outside System32
id: 7c5b2f0e-6a3d-4f7e-9e2a-1d5c8a3b9e11
status: experimental
description: Detects Windows service installation where the service binary name collides with a legitimate Windows binary (spoolsv, prntwsv, wndprbe, svchost, lsass, audiodg, etc.) but the ImagePath resides outside trusted system directories (System32/SysWOW64/Program Files). This is the StrongPity / PROMETHIUM persistence pattern documented by Microsoft and Cisco Talos.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0056/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/promethium-extends-with-strongpity3/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1543.003
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.t1036.004
  - attack.g0056
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: system
selection:
  Provider_Name: 'Service Control Manager'
  EventID: 7045
masq_filename:
  ImagePath|endswith:
    - '\spoolsv.exe'
    - '\prntwsv.exe'
    - '\wndprbe.exe'
    - '\svchost.exe'
    - '\services.exe'
    - '\lsass.exe'
    - '\winlogon.exe'
    - '\csrss.exe'
    - '\audiodg.exe'
    - '\taskhostw.exe'
    - '\wuauclt.exe'
    - '\smss.exe'
    - '\dllhost.exe'
filter_trusted_paths:
  ImagePath|contains:
    - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
    - 'C:\Windows\SysWOW64\'
    - 'C:\Program Files\'
    - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
user_writable_path:
  ImagePath|contains:
    - '\AppData\'
    - '\ProgramData\'
    - '\Users\Public\'
    - '\Temp\'
    - '\Roaming\'
condition: selection and masq_filename and user_writable_path and not filter_trusted_paths
fields:
  - Computer
  - ServiceName
  - ImagePath
  - AccountName
falsepositives:
  - Third-party software bundled with portable copies of system utilities (rare, generally allowlistable by ImagePath SHA256)
level: high
ucs.rationale: PROMETHIUM has consistently used service-name and binary-name collisions (e.g. wndprbe.exe, prntwsv.exe — near-clones of Windows spooler binaries) installed from %AppData%\Roaming or %ProgramData%, exactly because most generic 'service installed' alerts ignore ImagePath. By gating on (collision against the known Windows binary list OR a display-name like 'Print Spooler/Windows Defender') AND (Imag

_field_issues.message: `Spooler` is not a canonical column of DeviceEvents, Update.
`Windows` is not a canonical column of DeviceEvents, Update.
`Defender` is not a canonical column of DeviceEvents, Update.
`Audio` is not a canonical column of DeviceEvents, Update.
`Background` is not a canonical column of DeviceEvents, Update.
`Service` is not a canonical column of DeviceEvents, Update. Did you mean `DeviceId`?

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Spooler` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent, Update.
`Windows` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent, Update.
`Defender` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent, Update.
`Audio` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent, Update.
`Background` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent, Update.
`Service` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent, Update. Did you mean `ServiceSid`?
